name: Bandit (Python Security Linter)

auth.notes: Local static analysis tool; no auth

performance.notes: Static analysis speed; ~1-10s per project depending on size

agent_readiness.notes: Python AST-based security linter. bandit -r src/. Checks: hardcoded passwords/IPs, SQL injection, command injection (subprocess/os.system), XML parsing vulnerabilities, use of weak crypto (MD5, DES), assert statements in non-test code, pickle/yaml unsafe load. Severity: LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH. Confidence levels. -s B101 to skip tests. JSON output for CI integration. .bandit config file. Pre-commit hook integration. Part of SAST pipeline alongside Semgrep.

gotchas: High false positive rate for some checks (adjust with nosec or -s)
Only finds known patterns — not logic bugs
AST-based so won't catch runtime security issues
Some tests (B101 assert) need tuning for test-heavy codebases
best_when: Python codebase security scanning in CI/CD pipelines
Finding common security anti-patterns (hardcoded creds, SQL injection, weak crypto)
Pre-commit security gate for Python repos
Compliance requirements for static security analysis
avoid_when: Need deep semantic analysis (Semgrep rules more flexible)
Expecting it to catch all vulnerabilities (complement with other tools)
summary: Bandit is the standard Python security linter. bandit -r src/ scans for hardcoded passwords, SQL injection, command injection, weak crypto, unsafe deserialization. AST-based. LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH severity. JSON output for CI. Pre-commit integration. Part of Python SAST pipeline alongside Semgrep. High false positive rate — tune with nosec comments or skip flags.
